other_observers: Brenda Wright first noticed the bird at the suet feeder.
duration: At least 30 minutes over two day period multiple times.
description: All bright red with black tail and wings with some yellow under the wing and at the lower edge of wing on the body. Bill was gray and not thick and heavy and much like the bill of a Western Tanager. The tanager did sing on several occasions. The song was somewhat like a robin's song. On the 25th the tanager spend nearly the entire day in the yard.
photographer: Photographer: B Wright, B Wright, B Wright
